scoring_calibrations.reason: Italo's gamification needs are deliberately simple — activity milestones, badges, and counters only. No scratch cards, lucky draws, or complex redemption games. Score elevation is expected for rail/transport clients.

Italo does not require open SQL queries (F-12-09) or cross-market analytics rollup (F-12-12) — the two primary D-12 gap drivers. Score elevation is expected for single-market, single-brand clients without data science teams.

Oracle SOA middleware uplift applied (64%→72% baseline), but Italo has 5 non-Adobe custom integrations behind SOA (Sitecore, Navitaire, Amex, Azure, SAP BI). Each requires bespoke development. D-13 remains the lowest-scoring domain.

verification_failures.resolution: Reward management endpoints confirmed via issuals and redemptions APIs. Create endpoint not directly found in docs. Downgraded N→P. Open question raised for Capillary PS.

pattern_updates: Rail/transport clients will almost always trigger RF-04 (transport fraud) and have lower D-13 scores due to booking engine integrations (Navitaire-class systems) requiring custom connectors.

Oracle SOA central middleware consistently enables RF-03 mitigation. When SOA or equivalent middleware hub is confirmed, D-13 baseline can be shifted from 64% to 72%, but individual system builds behind SOA still count against domain score.
Adobe Campaign and Braze exemption from RF-10 is now confirmed native WIP — flag this proactively for any client using either ESP to avoid unnecessary deductions.

Anniversary-based qualifying periods (RF-05) are common in European loyalty programs. Confirm native Capillary support for per-member anniversary period config early in the engagement — this is a blocker-level question for SDD authoring.
GDPR lawful basis (F-15-03) should be flagged for ALL EU/EEA clients as a required Capillary PS pre-engagement question. The TRAI consent API is not a GDPR substitute.

Per-period benefit allotment with cancellation restore (RF-08) is a structurally difficult pattern for Capillary. Always requires: (a) userEntityTrackers for quota counting, (b) custom Connect+ workflow for cancellation restore. Flag as High effort in all future analyses.
notes: Atlassian MCP unavailable (server not initialised). Mermaid MCP unavailable (502 Bad Gateway). Both had no impact on analysis quality — BRD source was local PDF + CLAUDE.md, and diagrams were not required. Capillary Docs MCP fully operational — 118 verifications across 15 domains. 5 features downgraded: F-04-11 (corporate auto-demotion, X confirmed), F-06-02 (allotment restore, X confirmed), F-06-04 (Navitaire connector, X confirmed), F-07-23 (A/B testing, G confirmed), F-10-01 (reward/create, N→P downgraded).

new_features_discovered.description: Tier maintenance on previous tiers (bring-forward logic)
new_features_discovered.evidence: Known SessionM product gap requiring custom CAG Tier Confluence equivalent. No native Capillary mechanism for evaluating maintenance against previous tier criteria.
No native escrow mechanism in Capillary. Points credited immediately on transaction processing. Requires separate escrow point account + Connect+ delayed transfer.
Multiple point accounts possible but per-year isolation is non-standard. Rolling expiry with custom validity windows may suffice.
maxAmount or custom rule needed. Configuration-level but requires explicit setup.

new_features_discovered.description: Anomaly detection dashboards (Frequent Transaction, Multi-Accounting, Shared Accounts)
new_features_discovered.evidence: Capillary has 12 native fraud rules and fraud_details on customer. Does not have equivalent pre-built anomaly dashboards with store-level patterns.
partnerProgram APIs exist but custom mapping needed for CapitaStar data model.

new_features_discovered.description: KrisFlyer airline miles conversion with annual cap
new_features_discovered.evidence: No native airline miles connector. Requires custom Biz Layer integration + userEntityTrackers for 30K annual cap.

scoring_calibrations.reason: CAG has 3 non-standard tier mechanics: bring-forward logic requiring previous tier evaluation (X), staff tiers with manual entry (P), and ANS tracking per tier (P). Score depression consistent with RWS pattern for complex tier clients.

Escrow 7-day hold (X) and per-program-year point accounts (P) are non-standard requirements. Earning cap is configurable but adds complexity. Score depression driven by airport-specific financial controls.

8+ external systems with CapitaStar, KrisFlyer, CPMS, ChangiPay all requiring custom connectors. Biz Layer middleware role unconfirmed — if confirmed as hub, D-13 baseline would uplift to 72%. Airport clients have uniquely diverse integration ecosystems.

verification_failures.resolution: Verified via customer-v11 spec which contains transaction/add and customer paths. The v1.1 customer add endpoint is confirmed to exist through the spec but direct search did not surface it.
Consistent with Italo and RWS findings. Reward management operates through coupon series APIs rather than dedicated reward/create endpoint. Downgraded N→P.

pattern_updates: Airport/retail clients (CAG-class) have uniquely diverse integration ecosystems — partner loyalty (CapitaStar), airline miles (KrisFlyer), parking (CPMS), payment (ChangiPay), and multiple eCommerce channels — driving D-13 below baseline even with middleware.

Points escrow / delayed credit is a NEW structural pattern not seen in rail (Italo) or resort (RWS) clients. Airport programs may require hold periods due to high-volume transient customer transactions and tenant fraud risk. Flag escrow requirement proactively for airport/travel retail clients.

Per-program-year point account isolation is a variant of hard points reset (RF-13) but more nuanced — members need period-separated accounts rather than a clean wipe. Rolling expiry may be a simpler Capillary-native alternative; always confirm with client before defaulting to per-PY isolation.

Tier maintenance on previous tiers (bring-forward) is an emerging pattern seen first in CAG. Similar to RWS soft landing but more complex — involves evaluating maintenance against PREVIOUS tier criteria, not just current. This is a distinct capability gap from standard slab renewal.

Fixed fiscal year (Apr-Mar) for qualifying period does NOT trigger RF-05 (anniversary). Only per-member rolling 12-month periods from join date trigger RF-05. Calendar/fiscal year cycles are handled by standard Capillary program period config.

v3 promotions review endpoint (POST /v3/promotions/{id}/review) now confirmed as single-level approval mechanism across 3 clients (RWS, CAG). This is native and sufficient for most governance requirements. Multi-step hierarchical approval remains a gap.
notes: Capillary Docs MCP fully operational. 16 search queries across all priority groups. Key verifications: slab/manualSlabAdjustment, pointsExpirySchedule, userEntityTrackers, webhooks v3, promotions v3 review, badges full suite, milestones/targetGroups, leaderboards, partnerProgram APIs, coupon full suite, manual currency allocation, unified audits, subscription management, fraud_details. F-10-01 (reward/create) NOT FOUND — consistent with prior analyses. Lucky draw confirmed G-level (3rd consecutive analysis). This is the 3rd analysis — calibration report advisory at 5th.

scoring_calibrations.reason: RWS has 3 non-standard tier mechanics: soft landing conditional logic (X), invitation-only Pinnacle requiring TIER_D event (P), and Track Tier C special account (max 1pt, no expiry). All three create capability gaps vs Capillary native slab management. Score depression is expected for integrated resort clients with multi-condition tier rules.

GAP-02 approval workflow requirement (added March 2026) significantly degrades D-07. No multi-step forms-based approval exists in Capillary Engage+ or Loyalty+; must be procedural (MVP) or custom (Phase 2). This is a structural capability gap, not a configuration gap.

RWS wishlist includes lucky draws and scratch cards (both G-level, NOT FOUND in Capillary Docs MCP). Badges and milestones are confirmed native via full /api_gateway/v1/badges/* suite. Score depression driven by wishlist gamification items; if wishlist items are descoped post-MVP, D-11 would score ~92%.

verification_failures.resolution: Subscription management APIs confirmed (v2 + customer-v11). CONF_ENABLE_PII_DELETION not surfaced in Capillary Docs MCP but confirmed as known Capillary feature from prior Italo analysis. PDPA erasure flagged YELLOW (RF-02), not RED.
Both lucky draw and scratch card returned no results. G-level confirmed for both. These are wishlist items only; not MVP BRD requirements. GAP-05 logged.

pattern_updates: Integrated resort / entertainment clients (RWS-class) often have complex tier soft landing requirements due to multi-condition demotion rules. Always check for conditional downgrade logic early — this is a structural Capillary gap requiring tracker + Connect+ + Neo orchestration.

Per-member anniversary periods (RF-05) are not unique to European programs — RWS NGLP uses 365-day window from tier JOIN date. This is now confirmed as common across diverse geographies. Confirm calendar year option early as it removes RF-05 deduction entirely.

Approval workflow for campaigns (GAP-02) is an emerging client requirement in APAC (RWS) and should be flagged proactively for all clients with formal governance processes. Capillary has no native multi-step approver flow — always confirm if procedural RACI is acceptable for MVP.

Lucky draws and scratch cards are NOT in Capillary's native gamification suite (G-level confirmed by two independent searches). Badges, milestones, and leaderboards are native. Do not over-promise gamification for wishlist items — flag G-level and recommend 3rd-party integration for post-MVP.

RWS Middleware as central hub triggers RF-03 mitigation even when multiple downstream systems exist behind it. The key criterion is single integration point to Capillary, not the number of systems behind that point. This pattern is consistent with Oracle SOA (Italo).

SessionM migration clients consistently score RF-11 at −3% (not −5%). Data model is more structured than legacy CRM platforms (Siebel, Oracle Loyalty). Always apply the tiered deduction correctly: SM = −3%, Siebel/legacy = −5%.

PDPA (Singapore) is meaningfully different from GDPR: RF-01 does not fire, RF-02 is YELLOW not RED, and consent management APIs cover the primary PDPA obligations. Always distinguish PDPA clients from GDPR clients in compliance scoring.
notes: Atlassian MCP unavailable (server not initialized). Mermaid MCP unavailable (502 Bad Gateway). Both had no impact on analysis quality. Capillary Docs MCP fully operational — 16 searches across Priority 1, 2, and 3 verification groups. BRD PDF unreadable (poppler-utils not installed); all BRD data sourced from SDD v0.1 (2026-03-05) which was generated from BRD v1.2. Key verifications confirmed: userEntityTrackers, slab/manualSlabAdjustment, pointsExpirySchedule, subscriptions, webhooks (v3), promo codes, badge suite, referrals, coupon APIs, bulk currency allocate. Lucky draws and scratch cards confirmed G-level (NOT FOUND). GAP-01 (soft landing) and GAP-02 (approval workflow) are the primary RED gaps.
